recent_decisions.title: Honorary Freedom of the City awarded to Mr. Richard Brock
recent_decisions.political_summary: Council unanimously awarded Honorary Freeman status to Mr. Richard Brock for his WWII service and contributions to the Bay Veterans. No votes against.
key_data_points: Unanimous approval by council
Awarded for WWII service and Bay Veterans contributions
Presented with a scroll and subscribed to the Roll of Honorary Freeman

motions.text: That in pursuance of Section 249 of the Local Government Act 1972, the Freedom of the City of Lancaster be conferred on Mr Richard Brock in recognition of his service in World War II.

recent_decisions.title: Premises licence modified with stricter conditions and DPS removed
recent_decisions.political_summary: The council modified the Bombay Balti Restaurant's licence to include stricter right-to-work checks and CCTV requirements, and removed the Designated Premises Supervisor due to repeated illegal employment. The decision was unanimous with no recorded vote.
key_data_points: £85,000 in unpaid civil penalties for illegal employment
4 enforcement visits (2015, 2018, 2021, 2025) with illegal workers found
New owner claims compliance with right-to-work checks
CCTV installation required within 1 month

motions.text: The Sub-Committee decides to modify the premises licence to include additional conditions and remove Mr Nazrul Khan as the Designated Premises Supervisor.

recent_decisions.title: Endorsed Fees and Charges Policy for 2026/27
recent_decisions.political_summary: Cabinet unanimously approved the Fees and Charges Policy for 2026/27, including freezing garden waste charges and introducing new planning fees. Car parking charges will be consulted on before final decisions.
key_data_points: Garden waste collection charges frozen for 2026/27
New fees introduced in Planning & Climate Change
Car parking charges to be consulted on
Inflationary increases applied to other fees

motions.text: That the recommendations, as set out in the report, be approved.

recent_decisions.title: Recommended 2.99% Council Tax Increase
recent_decisions.political_summary: Cabinet unanimously recommended a 2.99% increase in the Lancaster City Council element of Council Tax, raising Band D from £264.30 to £272.20. The proposal will go to public consultation.
recent_decisions.financial_context: Increase in Band D Council Tax
key_data_points: 2.99% increase in Council Tax
Band D rises from £264.30 to £272.20
Public consultation scheduled for 3 February
Final decision to be made on 25 February

motions.text: That the recommendations, as set out in the report, be approved.

recent_decisions.title: Note interim changes to executive arrangements
recent_decisions.political_summary: Council noted interim changes to Cabinet responsibilities while the Deputy Leader is absent and an addition to executive delegations for Housing and Property.
key_data_points: Interim changes to Cabinet responsibilities
Addition to executive delegations for Housing and Property

recent_decisions.title: Agreed to note DfT consultation on taxi and PHV licensing
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee unanimously agreed to note the Department for Transport's consultation on local authority responsibility for taxi and private hire vehicle licensing and approved the draft response, delegating minor edits to the Licensing Manager.
key_data_points: Consultation noted and draft response approved
Delegation to Licensing Manager for minor edits
Unanimous agreement by committee members

motions.text: That the Department for Transport (DfT) consultation on making all local transport authorities (LTAs) in England responsible for taxi and private hire vehicle (PHV) licensing be noted.
That Council Business Committee agree the draft response to consultation questions as set out in the report.
That Council Business Committee delegate the Licensing Manager to make minor alterations to the response and submit the final version of the response to the DfT.

recent_decisions.title: Approved draft response to National Planning Policy Framework with amendment
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee unanimously approved the draft response to the National Planning Policy Framework consultation, including an amendment to question 67a regarding affordable housing delivery, allowing for commuted sums in certain cases.
key_data_points: Draft response approved with amendment to question 67a
Amendment allows for commuted sums for affordable housing in certain cases
Unanimous agreement by committee members

motions.text: That the consultation responses relating to the proposed National Planning Policy Framework be submitted as per the draft agreed by the Planning Policy Cabinet Advisory Group with the answer to question 67a substituted with the following: "As a matter of current Local Plan policy, the Council will seek to secure on-site affordable housing delivery. But policy is a matter of guidance, not axiomatic, and where the Council sees advantage in a particular case for funding off-site affordable housing,

recent_decisions.title: Approved response to Forest of Bowland National Landscape consultation
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee unanimously agreed to note the report and submit the proposed response to the Forest of Bowland National Landscape Draft Management Plan consultation.
key_data_points: Response to Forest of Bowland consultation approved
Draft response reviewed by Planning Policy Cabinet Advisory Group
Unanimous agreement by committee members

motions.text: That the report be noted and that the responses to the consultation questions as proposed are considered and submitted as the Council’s corporate response to the consultation.

recent_decisions.title: Draft response to NPPF consultation agreed with amendments
recent_decisions.political_summary: The group agreed to refine the response to the National Planning Policy Framework consultation, emphasizing on-site affordable housing as the default but allowing off-site funding in certain cases. The final response will go to the Council Business Committee.
key_data_points: On-site affordable housing is the default position
Off-site funding allowed in certain circumstances
Final response to go to Council Business Committee

motions.text: The draft response to the NPPF consultation was welcomed, with it being agreed that Officers would give further consideration to the wording of the answer to question 67a, in order to emphasise that whilst on-site provision of affordable housing was the default position, in certain circumstances off-site funding could be a possible alternative.

recent_decisions.title: Draft Authority Monitoring Report (AMR) 2024-25 welcomed
recent_decisions.political_summary: The group welcomed the draft Authority Monitoring Report for 2024-25, noting the 41% provision of affordable housing. Officers will clarify the calculation method for bus passenger numbers.
key_data_points: Draft AMR for 2024-25 presented
41% provision of affordable housing noted
Bus passenger numbers calculation to be clarified

recent_decisions.title: Draft response to Forest of Bowland National Landscape Management Plan 2026-2031 agreed
recent_decisions.political_summary: The group agreed to the draft response to the Forest of Bowland National Landscape Management Plan consultation, with no revisions proposed. Lancaster is one of seven authorities that will need to approve the final plan.
key_data_points: Draft response to Forest of Bowland Management Plan agreed
Consultation open from 19th January to 2nd March
Lancaster is one of seven authorities to approve the final plan

motions.text: The draft response to the Forest of Bowland National Landscape Management Plan 2026-2031 public consultation was agreed with no revisions proposed.

recent_decisions.title: Refused outline planning permission for 200 homes in Bolton-le-Sands
recent_decisions.political_summary: Councillors refused permission for 200 homes in Bolton-le-Sands, citing Green Belt protection. 10 voted for refusal, 3 against, 2 abstained.
key_data_points: 200 proposed dwellings including affordable housing
Site visit conducted by 9 councillors
11 public speakers opposed, 1 in favor

motions.text: That the application be refused on the grounds of Green Belt protection and expansion of built-up area.

recent_decisions.title: Approved 13 homes in Warton with Section 106 agreement
recent_decisions.political_summary: Councillors approved 13 homes in Warton with conditions including a Section 106 agreement. 14 voted in favor, none opposed.
key_data_points: 13 new dwellings approved
Section 106 agreement required
Contaminated land condition added

motions.text: That the application be approved subject to conditions including a contaminated land condition.

recent_decisions.title: Premises licence granted for Scale Hall Stores, Lancaster
recent_decisions.political_summary: The Licensing Sub-Committee granted a premises licence for Scale Hall Stores to sell alcohol off-site (07:00-23:00 daily) despite 14 objections. The decision was unanimous (3-0).
key_data_points: 14 objections received from residents
No objections from responsible authorities
Licence granted with standard conditions
Applicant has 10+ years of experience

motions.proposer: Councillors Martin Bottoms, Maria Deery, Martin Gawith
motions.text: Grant the application as applied for, subject to the conditions consistent with the operating schedule and mandatory conditions under the Licensing Act 2003.

recent_decisions.title: Panel notes budget and policy framework report
recent_decisions.political_summary: The Budget and Performance Panel noted the report on the General Fund revenue budget for 2026/27 to 2030/31, with no changes proposed to the budgetary framework. The panel agreed to add issues raised to their work programme.
key_data_points: No fundamental changes proposed to budget framework
Panel agreed to add issues to work programme

recent_decisions.title: Panel excludes press and public to discuss exempt appendices
recent_decisions.political_summary: The panel voted unanimously to exclude the press and public to discuss exempt appendices, citing potential disclosure of sensitive information.
key_data_points: Unanimous vote to exclude press and public
Decision based on potential disclosure of exempt information

motions.text: That, in accordance with Section 100A(4) of the Local Government Act, 1972, the press and public be excluded from the meeting to enable the Panel to consider Appendices C1 and C2 to the report, on the grounds that the information provided could involve the possible disclosure of exempt information as defined in paragraphs 2, 3 and 4 of Part 1 of Schedule 12A of that Act.

recent_decisions.title: Panel notes capital programme and strategy report
recent_decisions.political_summary: The panel noted the report on the Capital Programme and Strategy for 2026/27 to 2035/36, with no changes proposed to the strategy.
key_data_points: No changes proposed to capital strategy
Panel noted the report

recent_decisions.title: Panel notes treasury management strategy report
recent_decisions.political_summary: The panel noted the draft Treasury Management Strategy for 2026/27, which will be presented to Full Council for approval on 25 February 2026.
key_data_points: Strategy must align with budget proposals
No alternative options analysis available

recent_decisions.title: Adoption of Arnside and Silverdale National Landscape Management Plan
recent_decisions.political_summary: Cabinet approved the adoption of the Arnside and Silverdale National Landscape Management Plan to guide planning decisions and protect the area. Councillor Tyldesley abstained due to a conflict of interest.
key_data_points: Plan will act as a material consideration in planning decisions
Council fulfills statutory duty under Countryside and Rights of Way Act 2000
Plan guides management of protected landscape areas
Councillor Tyldesley abstained due to conflict of interest

motions.text: That the recommendation, as set out in the report, be approved.

recent_decisions.title: Approval of General Fund Revenue Budget 2026/27-2030/31
recent_decisions.political_summary: Cabinet approved the proposed General Fund revenue budget for 2026/27-2030/31, with Councillor Riches noting opposition to LGR transition fund allocation.
key_data_points: Budget must balance and fit with proposed Council Tax level
Little financial flexibility due to known commitments and Council Tax restrictions
Previous budget arrangements deemed reasonable
Councillor Riches opposed LGR transition fund allocation

motions.text: That the recommendations, as set out in the report, be approved.

recent_decisions.title: Approval of voluntary redundancy costs for RMS restructure
recent_decisions.political_summary: The committee approved voluntary redundancy costs for a restructure to improve service delivery in RMS. The exact financial details were exempt from publication.
recent_decisions.financial_context: Exempt from publication due to confidentiality
key_data_points: Restructure aimed to improve service delivery in RMS
Voluntary redundancy costs approved
Financial details exempt from publication

motions.text: That, in accordance with Section 100A (4) of the Local Government Act 1972, the press and public be excluded from the meeting for the following item of business on the grounds that it could involve the possible disclosure of exempt information as defined in paragraph 1 of Schedule 12A of that Act.
